string.text:
The Sharps 1874 rifle, a legendary single-shot firearm known for its accuracy and range, is a hallmark of American craftsmanship in the late 19th century. Originally designed for long-range hunting and target shooting, this rifle features a robust falling block action and a heavy octagonal barrel, ensuring precision and reliability. One of its distinguishing features is the capability to mount a scope, enhancing its already impressive accuracy for sharpshooting enthusiasts and professional marksmen alike. The Sharps 1874's historical significance and performance made it a favored choice among buffalo hunters and competitive shooters, solidifying its place in the annals of firearm history.
 
 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS: 
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] ZF-4 Scope

The .50-90 Sharps ammunition, introduced in the late 19th century, is a powerful cartridge designed for the Sharps 1874 rifle. With a .50 caliber bullet and 90 grains of black powder, it excels in big game hunting, capable of taking down large animals at long distances, and holds a legendary status in American firearm history.\n \n
